rulings.Case Title: TAL N. TAMIR ET AL VS. LEM HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Lem Holdings, Llc, Ciaran Harty And Patrick Harty
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 5, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFF TAL TAMIR, CELINE TAMIR MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Lem Holdings, Llc, Ciaran Harty And Patrick Harty Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Adjudication to the 11th cause of action of rescission is GRANTED as to the following: (1) Rescission of the Purchase Agreement; (2) Plaintiffs' return of the property (by way of rescinding deed) upon the return of the purchase funds by Defendant to Plaintiffs in the amount of $3,160,816.29; (3) An award of prejudgment interest in the amount of $729,997.02; (4) An award of consequential damages for funds paid by Plaintiffs to Defendant for improvements to the property in the amount of $93,5000; (5) An award of consequential damages for home loan (mortgage) payments, property taxes, and insurance incurred by Plaintiff in the amount of $557,664.20; (6) An award of CCP section 2394 punitive damages; and (7) an award of attorney fees to be established following order/judgment. Plaintiff shifted their burden and Defendant failed to raise a triable issue of material fact with competent admissible evidence. Plaintiffs did not waive their right to rescission, nor is their right to rescission estopped due to Plaintiffs delay in seeking the remedy. Plaintiff is ordered to prepare an order in accordance with CCP section 437c(g).=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TAL N. TAMIR ET AL VS. LEM HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Issue, Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions Against Lem Holdings, Llc, Ciaran Harty And Patrick Harty For Spoliation And Suppression Of Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 5, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFF TAL TAMIR, CELINE TAMIR Motion For Issue, Evidentiary And Monetary Sanctions Against Lem Holdings, Llc, Ciaran Harty And Patrick Harty For Spoliation And Suppression Of Evidence is GRANTED. The following facts are deemed established as issue sanctions: (1) DD failed to preserve evidence as required and, in fact, improperly destroyed / spoliated such evidence, (2) the evidence improperly destroyed and otherwise withheld including, but not limited to, text messages and emails with each other, agents/brokers for prospective purchasers of the home (135 Yukon Street, San Francisco, CA), and others concerning, among other things, defects with the construction of the Home, water/flooding damage to the property, disclosures made to others and not Plaintiffs, the subject real estate purchase transaction as well as Defendant's knowledge of adverse facts and information concerning the home which it concealed from Plaintiffs, and (3) the trier of fact shall draw an inference that those documents and this information would have been unfavorable to Defendants. Evidentiary sanctions granted and Defendants are barred from introducing any evidence or making any arguments in contravention of the foregoing issue sanctions. Monetary sanctions granted in the amount of $17,125 against Defendants jointly and severally to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA VS. CRAIG GOMEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or , In The Alternatve, Summary Adjudication; Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 5, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT CRAIG GOMEZ MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or , In The Alternatve, Summary Adjudication; Motion is GRANTED, subjection to opposition. Plaintiff attempted to file an opposition, which was rejected twice due to a typographical error on the filing. Defendant shifted their burden. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Summary Adjudication was not included in the Notice of Motion.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
